query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 6e250458).

## Issue to Resolve

Indentation rule inconsistently identifies issues with multiline string closing quotes between functions and properties
## Expected Behavior
Indentation rule treats properties and functions similarly when identifying issues with multiline string closing quotes.

## Observed Behavior
The rule is identifying issues with multiline string closing quotes inconsistently, when the only difference is whether the multiline string is returned from a function or is used as a property.

## Steps to Reproduce
Throws violation on last line, first char.
```kotlin
fun foo() = """
    <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
    <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
""".trimIndent()
```

Throws violation on second last line, first char.
```kotlin
fun foo2(): String {
    return """
        <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
        <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
    """.trimIndent()
}
```

Does not throw. The only difference between this and the first case is the change from `fun foo()` to `val foo`.
```kotlin
val foo = """
    <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
    <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
""".trimIndent()
```

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: N/A, using detekt wrapper, but also verified by adding failing test cases to `IndentationRuleTest`
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Gradle
* Version of Gradle used (if applicable): 8.1/8.1.1
* Operating System and version: Windows 11 22H2

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

> Throws violation on second last line, first char.

I can can not reproduce this finding on `fun foo2`. Like with `fun foo` the error is reported at the beginning of the line containing the closing quotes.

```
$ cat << EOF | ktlint-0.49.0 --stdin
> fun foo() = """
>     <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
>     <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
> """.trimIndent()
> 
> fun foo2(): String {
>     return """
>         <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
>         <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
>     """.trimIndent()
> }
> 
> val foo = """
>     <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
>     <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
> """.trimIndent()
> EOF
17:22:41.009 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
<stdin>:4:1: Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes (standard:indent)
<stdin>:10:1: Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes (standard:indent)
```

> Does not throw. The only difference between this and the first case is the change from fun foo() to val foo.
> ```
> val foo = """
>     <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
>     <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
> """.trimIndent()
> ```

This is indeed not consistent and needs to be fixed.
@3flex Can you please respond on previous comment? It would be more convenient for me to know whether a problem is to be fixed with example of `fun foo2()`.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

content.thinking:
Looking at this issue, it seems to be about the IndentationRule in ktlint that is inconsistently identifying issues with multiline string closing quotes between functions and properties.

Let me break down the issue:

1. For `fun foo() = """...`.trimIndent()` - rule throws violation on last line, first char
2. For `fun foo2(): String { return """...`.trimIndent() }` - rule throws violation on second last line, first char
3. For `val foo = """...`.trimIndent()` - rule does NOT throw (inconsistent behavior)

The issue is that properties (val foo) are being handled differently than functions when it comes to multiline string indentation validation.

Let me start by exploring the codebase to understand the structure and locate the relevant files.

content.text: I'll help you analyze and fix this issue with the IndentationRule in ktlint. Let's start by exploring the codebase to understand the current implementation and the root cause of the inconsistency.

 126â†’public class IndentationRule :
 127â†’    StandardRule(
 128â†’        id = "indent",
 129â†’        visitorModifiers =
 130â†’            setOf(
 131â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 132â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 133â†’                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 134â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 135â†’                ),
 136â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 137â†’                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 138â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 139â†’                ),
 140â†’                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 141â†’                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 142â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 143â†’                ),
 144â†’            ),
 145â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 146â†’            setOf(
 147â†’                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 148â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 149â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 150â†’            ),
 151â†’    ) {
 152â†’    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 153â†’    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 154â†’
 155â†’    private var line = 1
 156â†’
 157â†’    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 158â†’
 159â†’    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter

 161â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 162â†’        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 163â†’        indentConfig =
 164â†’            IndentConfig(
 165â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 166â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 167â†’            )
 168â†’        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 169â†’            stopTraversalOfAST()
 170â†’        }
 171â†’    }
 172â†’
 173â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 174â†’        node: ASTNode,
 175â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 176â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 177â†’    ) {
 178â†’        if (node.isRoot()) {
 179â†’            // File should not start with a whitespace
 180â†’            node
 181â†’                .nextLeaf()
 182â†’                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 183â†’                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 184â†’                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 185â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
 186â†’                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 187â†’                    }
 188â†’                }
 189â†’            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 190â†’        }
 191â†’
 192â†’        when {
 193â†’            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 194â†’                line++
 195â†’                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 196â†’                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 197â†’                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 198â†’                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 199â†’                    )
 200â†’                }
 201â†’                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 202â†’            }
 203â†’
 204â†’            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 205â†’                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 206â†’                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 207â†’                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 208â†’                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 209â†’                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 210â†’                startIndentContext(
 211â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 212â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 213â†’                )
 214â†’
 215â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 216â†’                visitValueArgument(node)
 217â†’
 218â†’            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 219â†’                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 220â†’
 221â†’            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 222â†’                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 223â†’                startIndentContext(node)
 224â†’
 225â†’            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 226â†’                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 227â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 228â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 229â†’                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 230â†’                startIndentContext(node)
 231â†’
 232â†’            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 233â†’                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 234â†’                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 235â†’                startIndentContext(
 236â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 237â†’                    childIndent = "",
 238â†’                )
 239â†’
 240â†’            node.elementType == IF ->
 241â†’                visitIf(node)
 242â†’
 243â†’            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 244â†’                visitLbrace(node)
 245â†’
 246â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 247â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 248â†’                startIndentContext(
 249â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 250â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 251â†’                )
 252â†’
 253â†’            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 254â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 255â†’                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 256â†’
 257â†’            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 258â†’                visitValueParameter(node)
 259â†’
 260â†’            node.elementType == FUN ->
 261â†’                visitFun(node)
 262â†’
 263â†’            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 264â†’                visitClass(node)
 265â†’
 266â†’            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 267â†’                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 268â†’
 269â†’            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 270â†’                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 271â†’
 272â†’            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 273â†’                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 274â†’                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 275â†’                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 276â†’                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 277â†’                ) {
 278â†’                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 279â†’                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 280â†’                    //    .bar[0] {
 281â†’                    //        "foobar"
 282â†’                    //    }
 283â†’                    startIndentContext(
 284â†’                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 285â†’                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 286â†’                    )
 287â†’                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 288â†’                    startIndentContext(node)
 289â†’                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 290â†’                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 291â†’                } else {
 292â†’                    startIndentContext(node)
 293â†’                }
 294â†’            }
 295â†’
 296â†’            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 297â†’                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 298â†’                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 299â†’
 300â†’            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 301â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 302â†’                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 303â†’
 304â†’            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 305â†’                visitWhen(node)
 306â†’
 307â†’            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 308â†’                visitWhenEntry(node)
 309â†’
 310â†’            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 311â†’                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 312â†’                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 313â†’
 314â†’            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 315â†’                visitKdoc(node)
 316â†’
 317â†’            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 318â†’                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 319â†’                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 320â†’
 321â†’            node.elementType == FOR ||
 322â†’                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 323â†’                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 324â†’
 325â†’            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 326â†’                visitLBracket(node)
 327â†’
 328â†’            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 329â†’                visitNullableType(node)
 330â†’
 331â†’            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 332â†’                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 333â†’
 334â†’            node.elementType == TRY ->
 335â†’                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 336â†’
 337â†’            else -> {
 338â†’                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 339â†’            }
 340â†’        }
 341â†’    }

 343â†’    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 344â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 345â†’            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 346â†’            //     val foo = foo(
 347â†’            //         parameterName =
 348â†’            //             "The quick brown fox "
 349â†’            //                .plus("jumps ")
 350â†’            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 351â†’            //     )
 352â†’            startIndentContext(
 353â†’                fromAstNode = node,
 354â†’                lastChildIndent = "",
 355â†’            )
 356â†’        }
 357â†’    }

 359â†’    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 360â†’        node
 361â†’            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 362â†’            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 363â†’                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 364â†’                val nextToAstNode =
 365â†’                    startIndentContext(
 366â†’                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 367â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 368â†’
 369â†’                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 370â†’                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 371â†’                    startIndentContext(
 372â†’                        fromAstNode = node,
 373â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 374â†’                        childIndent = "",
 375â†’                    )
 376â†’                }
 377â†’            }
 378â†’    }
 379â†’
 380â†’    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 381â†’        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 382â†’        node
 383â†’            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 384â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 385â†’                nextToAstNode =
 386â†’                    startIndentContext(
 387â†’                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 388â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 389â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 390â†’            }
 391â†’
 392â†’        node
 393â†’            .findChildByType(THEN)
 394â†’            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 395â†’            ?.nextLeaf()
 396â†’            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 397â†’                nextToAstNode =
 398â†’                    startIndentContext(
 399â†’                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 400â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 401â†’                        childIndent = "",
 402â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 403â†’            }
 404â†’        node
 405â†’            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 406â†’            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 407â†’            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 408â†’                nextToAstNode =
 409â†’                    startIndentContext(
 410â†’                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 411â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 412â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 413â†’            }
 414â†’        startIndentContext(
 415â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 416â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 417â†’            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 418â†’        )
 419â†’    }

 421â†’    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 422â†’        // Outer indent context
 423â†’        val rbrace =
 424â†’            requireNotNull(
 425â†’                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 426â†’            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 427â†’        startIndentContext(
 428â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 429â†’            toAstNode = rbrace,
 430â†’            firstChildIndent = "",
 431â†’            lastChildIndent = "",
 432â†’        )
 433â†’
 434â†’        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 435â†’        node
 436â†’            .treeParent
 437â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 438â†’            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 439â†’            ?.let { arrow ->
 440â†’                startIndentContext(
 441â†’                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 442â†’                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 443â†’                    lastChildIndent = "",
 444â†’                )
 445â†’                startIndentContext(
 446â†’                    fromAstNode = node,
 447â†’                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 448â†’                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 449â†’                )
 450â†’            }
 451â†’    }

 453â†’    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 454â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 455â†’            // val fieldExample =
 456â†’            //      LongNameClass {
 457â†’            //              paramA,
 458â†’            //              paramB,
 459â†’            //              paramC ->
 460â†’            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 461â†’            //      }
 462â†’            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 463â†’        } else {
 464â†’            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 465â†’            // val fieldExample =
 466â†’            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 467â†’            //                     paramB,
 468â†’            //                     paramC ->
 469â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 470â†’            //     }
 471â†’            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 472â†’                ?.let { callExpression ->
 473â†’                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 474â†’                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 475â†’                            " { "
 476â†’                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 477â†’                }
 478â†’                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 479â†’        }
 480â†’
 481â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 482â†’        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 483â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 484â†’            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null

 486â†’    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 487â†’        startIndentContext(
 488â†’            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 489â†’            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 490â†’            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 491â†’            childIndent = "",
 492â†’        )
 493â†’    }
 494â†’
 495â†’    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 496â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 497â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 498â†’        node
 499â†’            .findChildByType(EQ)
 500â†’            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 501â†’                nextToAstNode =
 502â†’                    startIndentContext(
 503â†’                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 504â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 505â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 506â†’            }
 507â†’
 508â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 509â†’            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 510â†’            //     fun process(
 511â†’            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 512â†’            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 513â†’            //     ): List<Output>
 514â†’            node
 515â†’                .findChildByType(COLON)
 516â†’                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 517â†’                    nextToAstNode =
 518â†’                        startIndentContext(
 519â†’                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 520â†’                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 521â†’                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 522â†’                }
 523â†’        }
 524â†’
 525â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 526â†’        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 527â†’        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 528â†’            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 529â†’            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 530â†’        ) {
 531â†’            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 532â†’                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 533â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 534â†’            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 535â†’        } else {
 536â†’            startIndentContext(
 537â†’                fromAstNode = node,
 538â†’                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 539â†’                childIndent = "",
 540â†’            )
 541â†’        }
 542â†’    }
 543â†’
 544â†’    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 545â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 546â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 547â†’        val eqOrBlock =
 548â†’            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 549â†’                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 550â†’        eqOrBlock?.let {
 551â†’            nextToAstNode =
 552â†’                startIndentContext(
 553â†’                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 554â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 555â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 556â†’        }
 557â†’
 558â†’        node
 559â†’            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 560â†’            ?.let { typeReference ->
 561â†’                nextToAstNode =
 562â†’                    startIndentContext(
 563â†’                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 564â†’                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 565â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 566â†’            }
 567â†’
 568â†’        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 569â†’        startIndentContext(
 570â†’            fromAstNode = node,
 571â†’            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 572â†’            childIndent = "",
 573â†’        )
 574â†’    }

 576â†’    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 577â†’        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 578â†’        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 579â†’        node
 580â†’            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 581â†’            ?.let { where ->
 582â†’                val typeConstraintList =
 583â†’                    requireNotNull(
 584â†’                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 585â†’                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 586â†’                require(
 587â†’                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 588â†’                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 589â†’                nextToAstNode =
 590â†’                    startIndentContext(
 591â†’                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 592â†’                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 593â†’                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 594â†’            }
 595â†’
 596â†’        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 597â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null) {
 598â†’            // Indent both constructor and super type list
 599â†’            nextToAstNode =
 600â†’                startIndentContext(
 601â†’                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 602â†’                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 603â†’                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 604â†’        } else {
 605â†’            node
 606â†’                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 607â†’                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 608â†’                    nextToAstNode =

 986â†’                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"

 998â†’    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {

1000â†’        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {

1004â†’
1005â†’        nextLeaf

1008â†’                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {

1027â†’    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {

1029â†’        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()

1031â†’            when {
1032â†’                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1033â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1034â†’                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1035â†’
1036â†’                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1037â†’                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1038â†’                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1039â†’
1040â†’                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent

1045â†’    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1046â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1047â†’        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1048â†’            SPACE -> {
1049â†’                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1050â†’                    emit(
1051â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1052â†’                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1053â†’                        true,
1054â†’                    )
1055â†’                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1056â†’                } else {
1057â†’                    nodeIndent
1058â†’                }
1059â†’            }
1060â†’
1061â†’            TAB -> {
1062â†’                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1063â†’                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1064â†’                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1065â†’                    emit(
1066â†’                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1067â†’                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1068â†’                        true,
1069â†’                    )
1070â†’                    indentConfig
1071â†’                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1072â†’                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1073â†’                } else {
1074â†’                    nodeIndent
1075â†’                }
1076â†’            }
1077â†’        }
1078â†’    }

1096â†’    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1097â†’        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1098â†’        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1099â†’            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1100â†’                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1101â†’                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1102â†’                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1103â†’                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1104â†’                }
1105â†’
1106â†’                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1107â†’                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1108â†’                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1109â†’                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1110â†’                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1111â†’                }
1112â†’
1113â†’                else -> ""
1114â†’            }
1115â†’        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1116â†’        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1117â†’            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1118â†’        } else {
1119â†’            ""
1120â†’        }

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue clearly. The problem is that the logic for determining `correctedExpectedIndent` has specific cases for:

1. `isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()` - returns `node.indent(false) + indentConfig.indent`
2. `isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()` - returns `node.indent(false) + indentConfig.indent`
3. `prevLeaf?.text == "\n"` - returns `""`
4. **The else case** - returns `expectedIndent` (which comes from the caller)

The issue is that property assignments are falling into case 4 (the else case), while function expressions are handled in case 2.

I need to add a method to detect property assignments and handle them similar to function expressions. Let me look for how the `isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression` method works to understand how to create a similar one for property assignments:

I've successfully identified and resolved the issue with inconsistent indentation rule behavior between functions and properties for multiline string closing quotes.### Root Cause
The issue was in the `StringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes` method in `IndentationRule.kt`. The method had specific handling for:
1. Function body expressions (`isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression`)
2. Return statements (`isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock`)

But property assignments were falling through to the default case, causing inconsistent behavior.

content.text:
## Summary

I have successfully analyzed and fixed the issue described in GitHub issue #2145. Here's what was accomplished:

### Problem Identified
The IndentationRule was inconsistently handling multiline string closing quotes between:
- Function expressions (`fun foo() = """..."""`)
- Function return statements (`return """..."""`) 
- Property assignments (`val foo = """..."""`)

Functions were correctly reporting violations on closing quotes, but properties were not being handled consistently.

### Root Cause
The issue was in the `StringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes` method. It had specific detection methods for function contexts but property assignments were falling through to the default case, causing inconsistent behavior.

### Solution Implemented

1. **Added new detection method**: Created `isRawStringLiteralPropertyAssignment()` following the same pattern as existing function detection methods

2. **Updated indentation logic**: Modified the `visitClosingQuotes` method to handle property assignments consistently with function expressions by adding:
   ```kotlin
   } else if (node.isRawStringLiteralPropertyAssignment()) {
       // Allow consistent behavior with function expressions
       node.indent(false).plus(indentConfig.indent)
   ```

3. **Added comprehensive tests**: Created test cases covering all three scenarios from the issue

4. **Updated changelog**: Documented the fix

### Files Modified
- `IndentationRule.kt` - Core fix implementation
- `IndentationRuleTest.kt` - Test cases for the fix
- `CHANGELOG.md` - Documentation of the fix

### Result
The fix ensures that properties and functions are now treated consistently when identifying issues with multiline string closing quotes. All three scenarios from the issue now report violations consistently, resolving the behavioral inconsistency described in the GitHub issue.

 453â†’    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 454â†’        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 455â†’            // val fieldExample =
 456â†’            //      LongNameClass {
 457â†’            //              paramA,
 458â†’            //              paramB,
 459â†’            //              paramC ->
 460â†’            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 461â†’            //      }
 462â†’            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 463â†’        } else {
 464â†’            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 465â†’            // val fieldExample =
 466â†’            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 467â†’            //                     paramB,
 468â†’            //                     paramC ->
 469â†’            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 470â†’            //     }
 471â†’            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 472â†’                ?.let { callExpression ->
 473â†’                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 474â†’                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 475â†’                            " { "
 476â†’                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 477â†’                }
 478â†’                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 479â†’        }

1045â†’    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {

1052â†’                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1053â†’                        true,
1054â†’                    )

1059â†’            }
1060â†’
1061â†’            TAB -> {

1064â†’                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {

1067â†’                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1068â†’                        true,
1069â†’                    )
1070â†’                    indentConfig
1071â†’                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1072â†’                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1073â†’                } else {
1074â†’                    nodeIndent
1075â†’                }

1171â†’        val lastChildIndent: String,

1177â†’        val nodes: String

1298â†’                                )

1301â†’                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1302â†’                                    )

1319â†’                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1320â†’                                        )
1321â†’                                    }
1322â†’                                }
1323â†’                            }
1324â†’                        }
1325â†’                    }
1326â†’            }

}", "new_string": "    @Nested
    inner class `Issue 2145 - Given multiline string closing quotes inconsistency between functions and properties` {
        @Test
        fun `Given a function expression with multiline string should report violation on closing quotes`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo() = $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
                    <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
                    <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
                $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun foo() = $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
                    <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
                    <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(4, 1, "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a function with return statement and multiline string should report violation on closing quotes`() {
            val code =
                """
                fun foo2(): String {
                    return $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
                        <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
                        <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()
                }
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                fun foo2(): String {
                    return $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
                        <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
                        <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
                        $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()
                }
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(5, 1, "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }

        @Test
        fun `Given a property with multiline string should consistently report violation on closing quotes`() {
            val code =
                """
                val foo = $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
                    <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
                    <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
                $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()
                """.trimIndent()
            val formattedCode =
                """
                val foo = $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
                    <!--suppress XmlUnusedNamespaceDeclaration -->
                    <manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"/>
                    $MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE.trimIndent()
                """.trimIndent()
            indentationRuleAssertThat(code)
                .hasLintViolation(4, 1, "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes")
                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
        }
    }

    private companion object {
        val INDENT_STYLE_TAB =
            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY to PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab
    }
}"}

1â†’# Changelog
 2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4â†’
 5â†’## Unreleased
 6â†’
 7â†’### Added
 8â†’
 9â†’### Removed
10â†’
11â†’### Fixed
12â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
13â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
14â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
15â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
16â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
17â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
18â†’
19â†’### Changed
20â†’* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers
21â†’
22â†’## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
23â†’
24â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
25â†’
26â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
27â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
28â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
29â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
30â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
